basics.name: Mario Rico Ibáñez
basics.label: Telecommunications Engineer | Machine Learning Researcher
basics.summary: Telecommunications Engineer pursuing a Master's in Computer Science at EPFL, with a Bachelor's degree in Telecommunications Engineering from UPV. Specialized in Deep Reinforcement Learning for UAV networks and telecommunications optimization, as well as Generative AI and Large Language Models.

work.name: Xcert AI
work.summary: Developing Generative AI solutions for aerospace certification and compliance processes.
highlights: Developing Generative AI solutions for aerospace certification and compliance processes
Building modules to process complex PDF documents and high-precision aerial and satellite images for aerodrome certification
Implementing LLM-based systems to analyze and verify compliance of runways, taxiways, and emergency infrastructure
Applying clustering analysis, image processing, and MCP integration for automated compliance verification
Collaborating with multidisciplinary aerospace system engineering and development teams at a CERN/ESA-backed startup

work.name: Instituto de Telecomunicaciones y Aplicaciones Multimedia
work.summary: Developed 5G/6G wireless communication simulators and optimized network strategies using Deep Reinforcement Learning.
highlights: Developed 5G/6G multi-carrier, massive MIMO, GPU-based system-level wireless communication simulator using PyTorch
Utilized Deep Reinforcement Learning strategies to optimize the placement of UAV Base Stations

Investigated the applicability of Deep Reinforcement Learning techniques in telecommunications across varied scenarios, including singular 4G layer, multi-layer environments, and several distributions of users
Supervised by Prof. Valery Naranjo Ornedo and Dr. David López Pérez

work.name: Computer Vision & Behaviour Analysis Lab
work.summary: Developed SOTA text models for Emotion Recognition and researched LLM explainability.
highlights: Developed SOTA text models for Multilabel Emotion Recognition within a psychology context
Conducted research on Deep Learning for Emotion Recognition, comparing Encoder-only Transformers (BERT, RoBERTa) with Decoder-only Transformers (Llama-2, Mistral, Qwen)
Explored the explainability of Large Language Models in the context of Emotion Recognition

volunteer.summary: Served a full academic year as a delegate for the IAESTE organization, focusing on securing international job placements for students.
highlights: Actively engaged with both students and employers, facilitating successful job matches and supporting students throughout their application processes

awards.title: Honors at Upper Secondary School
awards.summary: Achieved Honors in the last year

awards.title: Top 3 Student
awards.summary: Top 3 out of 200+ students in Telecommunications Engineering

awards.title: High Performance Group
awards.summary: Participant in the High Performance Group with English teaching

publications.name: Mejorando el Análisis de Emociones en texto mediante Llama 2
publications.publisher: XLI Congreso Anual de la Sociedad Española de Ingeniería Biomédica
publications.summary: Rico Ibáñez, Mario; del Amor, Rocío; Naranjo, Valery. In: XLI Congreso Anual de la Sociedad Española de Ingeniería Biomédica. Valencia: Universitat Politècnica de València, 2023. Pp. 670-673. ISBN: 978-84-17853-76-1

publications.name: Optimizing Aerial Base Station Positioning Using Deep Reinforcement Learning in UAV Networks
publications.summary: Rico Ibáñez, Mario; Akhtarshenas, Azim; López-Pérez, David; Geraci, Giovanni.

projects.name: Adversarial Attacks on Image-to-Image Generative Models
projects.summary: Investigated the robustness of Image-to-Image generative models against adversarial attacks.
highlights: Investigated the robustness of Image-to-Image generative models against adversarial attacks, such as Automatic Projected Gradient Descent, analyzing vulnerabilities and defenses (e.g., RobustCLIP)
Developed adversarial strategies targeting models based on Diffusion Models, specifically assessing the stability of embeddings
Conducted extensive experiments evaluating the performance impacts of adversarial perturbations across datasets
Supervised by Prof. Volkan Cevher and Elias Abad Rocamora

projects.name: Evolution Strategies for Deep Reinforcement Learning Pretraining
projects.summary: Investigated Evolution Strategies as standalone RL methods and pretraining mechanisms for gradient-based algorithms.
highlights: Investigated Evolution Strategies as standalone RL methods and pretraining mechanisms for gradient-based algorithms (DQN, PPO)
Conducted extensive experiments across Flappy Bird, Breakout, and MuJoCo environments
Analyzed trade-offs between gradient-free and gradient-based methods in terms of convergence speed and performance
Demonstrated that ES provides effective early exploration but faces scalability limitations
Collaborative project with Swiss Data Science Center

projects.name: Chatsplaining: Intelligent STEM Tutoring System
projects.summary: Developed an AI tutor by fine-tuning Qwen3-0.6B-Base language model for EPFL-related STEM questions.
highlights: Developed an AI tutor by fine-tuning Qwen3-0.6B-Base language model for EPFL-related STEM questions
Implemented four model variants: supervised fine-tuning (SFT), quantization (W4A8/W8A8), retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and direct preference optimization (DPO)
Designed two-stage curriculum learning combining instruction-following with domain-specific MCQA fine-tuning
Built RAG system with custom-trained embeddings and FAISS vector store
Achieved significant performance improvements across multiple benchmarks (HellaSwag, MathQA, MedMCQA, MMLU, SciQ)

projects.name: Impact of Whole-Slide Image Resolution on Pathology Models
projects.summary: Systematically analyzed how changes in Whole-Slide Image (WSI) resolution affect the performance of histopathological foundation models.
highlights: Systematically analyzed how changes in Whole-Slide Image (WSI) resolution affect the performance of histopathological foundation models
Implemented a preprocessing pipeline to generate and validate tiles across varying magnification levels (5x, 10x, 20x, 40x)
Utilized Multiple-Instance Learning (MIL) techniques to aggregate embeddings from the UNI model
Conducted ablation studies on three breast histology datasets (BACH, BRACS, BreakHis)
